response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web-backend engineer with a broad polyglot background, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency — an absolute prerequisite that is entirely absent from his profile. His primary expertise lies in TypeScript, Node.js, and web/cloud technologies, with only marginal C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in a thesis project. There is no evidence of Linux kernel development, embedded firmware work, LAN-switch or Layer-2/3 networking experience, or any of the required security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, tamper detection). The technical stack and domain are a near-complete mismatch.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, embedded, and infrastructure domains demonstrates adaptability and long-term commitment to the craft
Some embedded and low-level systems exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency async I/O in DLang at Sociomantic, and Linux usage across multiple roles
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, code quality) would translate well to any rigorous engineering team
concerns: Japanese language proficiency is listed as a hard requirement; the candidate lists only English, Romanian, and basic German — this alone is a disqualifying gap
No evidence of Linux kernel development, source-code modification, or embedded firmware work; experience is almost entirely at the application and services layer
Zero demonstrated knowledge of the required security domain (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange) or networking domain (L2/L3, switch chips, NETCONF, SNMP)

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
